audit.title: YCM Owner Payment-to-Accounting Continuity Audit
audit.scope: Successful owner payments, duplicate/replay handling, derived GL projection failure recovery, and payment reversal projection

authorities.requirements: Approved YCM Continuity Correction and Bank Rules/HOA Accounting R2 mandates
authorities.code: YourCondoManager branch fix/ycm-gl-canonical-shape-repair at base 05ae79afcfa98d14f4a6cb8a7986b9c2db855e83 plus this repair
authorities.data: Production owner ledger, payment transactions, webhook records, and derived GL inspected read-only using aggregate evidence
authorities.provider: Stripe signed webhook contract; no new provider action performed
authorities.deployment: Existing staged-release and protected production release gates

actors.name: Association owner
actors.role: Pays an association obligation and reads payment history

actors.name: Association financial operator
actors.role: Reviews financial alerts, ledgers, and statements within association scope

actors.name: YCM payment and accounting automation
actors.role: Validates provider events, writes canonical payment truth, derives journals, and retries gaps

actors.name: Stripe
actors.role: External payment provider and signed-event authority

capabilities.name: Settle an owner payment through ledger, accounting, statements, and receipt
ui_entrypoints: Owner Portal ledger and receipt history

capabilities.name: Deduplicate and safely replay a payment event
Detect and recover a missed accounting projection
Project a refund or chargeback reversal into accounting

workflows.name: Settled owner payment reaches accounting
workflows.trigger: Stripe sends a signed success event after an owner payment settles
preconditions: Association, unit, person, and payment identity resolve in one association
Association is in the GL cohort for journal projection
terminal_outcomes: One balanced cash and Accounts Receivable journal exists
Owner receipt is sent or safely retryable
invariants: GL Accounts Receivable equals owner subledger
Projection failure never erases payment truth
happy_path: Insert or resolve canonical owner-ledger payment
Reconcile and project canonical GL journal
alternate_paths: Platform Connect completes the workflow when the per-association webhook is delayed or absent
ACH may remain pending until the provider success event
failure_paths: Invalid provider event fails before mutation
GL failure preserves ledger truth and enters WF-PAY-003
Receipt failure clears its sent marker so retry remains possible

inputs: Signed Stripe event and trusted local payment transaction
retention: Financial records and supporting evidence follow the approved minimum seven-year policy

notifications.audience: Owning person for the paid unit
notifications.channel: Email plus Owner Portal receipt history
notifications.failure_handling: Sent marker is cleared on provider failure so retry remains possible; failures are logged

authorization: Owner Portal session and server-side payment-route checks
tenant_scope: Association IDs must agree across signed metadata, transaction, unit, and owner-ledger write
privacy: No payment credentials are stored in audit evidence

retry: Provider replay and five-minute continuity sweep re-run idempotent projection
compensation: Payment truth remains in owner ledger while GL is derived and recoverable
manual_recovery: Protected canonical GL repair after read-only audit, snapshot, and approval reference

evidence.claim: The canonical payment writer owns immediate GL projection for new and duplicate payment identities
Signed Connect success posts ledger, marks transaction succeeded, and invokes the idempotent receipt fallback
Read-only production RCA bounded the pre-repair state to one missing $330 payment journal and $330 Accounts Receivable drift
evidence.locator: aggregate production canonical GL audit; private rows omitted

evidence.claim: Projection alerts exclude private payment identifiers and resolve only after success

workflows.name: Duplicate or replayed payment heals without duplication
workflows.trigger: A duplicate, cross-path, concurrent, or manually replayed provider event arrives
preconditions: An owner-ledger payment may already exist for the same association and canonical identity
terminal_outcomes: Exactly one owner-ledger economic payment exists
Accounting projection is checked or healed
invariants: Concurrent writes converge on one payment
GL journal legs remain exactly once
Replay can heal but cannot duplicate
happy_path: Resolve exact event or canonical payment identity
alternate_paths: The same payment may arrive through per-association webhook, platform Connect, autopay, or payout reconciliation
failure_paths: Projection failure enters the alert and continuity-sweep workflow without changing payment identity

inputs: Provider event ID and canonical payment identity
writes: No new owner-ledger row on duplicate
Only missing canonical GL legs may be inserted
retention: Original financial event and source linkage remain retained

notifications.failure_handling: A duplicate intentionally does not generate a second receipt

validation: Association, identity, and exact reference comparison
privacy: Recovery logs use identifiers without raw provider payload
audit_trail: Existing owner-ledger row and provider event remain linked

logs: Existing-payment skip with source and identity
metrics: Projection sweep reconciled and failed counters
alerts: Projection failure alert only when healing fails

retry: Five-minute sweep retries independently of provider delivery
compensation: not-applicable — no duplicate economic write is created
manual_recovery: Protected canonical GL repair when persisted shape is noncanonical

evidence.claim: Exact, cross-path, concurrent, and payout replay scenarios remain exactly once and re-run projection
The receipt service uses a sent marker to suppress duplicate owner receipts

workflows.name: Projection failure alerts and self-recovers
workflows.trigger: GL reconcile, canonical-shape, database, or post-commit process failure prevents journal completion
preconditions: Association is in the enabled GL cohort
terminal_outcomes: One critical association-scoped alert is visible
Aggregate health shows an active failure
A later successful projection dismisses the alert
invariants: A GL failure is non-fatal to payment truth
One failing association does not abort peers
happy_path: Dismiss alert only after successful canonical sync
alternate_paths: A provider replay may recover before the scheduled sweep
failure_paths: Persistent failure leaves alert active and retries on later cycles
Alert persistence failure is logged and does not alter payment truth

inputs: Association ID and generic failure class
retention: The deterministic alert row is retained and dismissed rather than deleted

notifications.audience: Scoped association financial operators and platform operations
notifications.channel: Financial Alerts UI and aggregate platform health warning
notifications.failure_handling: The alert reopens on every failed cycle and resolves only after a successful projection

authorization: Financial Alerts route is admin-authenticated and association-scoped
validation: Only reconcile-failed and runtime-error classes are accepted
tenant_scope: Alert key and row carry the affected association ID
privacy: Generic message omits raw error, owner, unit, payment, amount, and provider details
audit_trail: Alert row and system dismissal actor

compensation: Owner ledger remains the financial authority until derived GL catches up
manual_recovery: Protected canonical repair when unexpected persisted journal shape blocks additive sync

evidence.claim: Reconcile/runtime failures remain non-fatal to payment truth and persist a critical alert
The continuity sweep isolates associations and retries only enabled cohorts
A deterministic private-data-minimized alert is created and resolves only after success
Admin health exposes only the aggregate active-failure count and Financial Alerts remains association-scoped

workflows.name: Refund or chargeback reversal reaches accounting
workflows.trigger: Stripe sends a signed successful refund or lost-dispute event
preconditions: Refund succeeded or dispute status is lost
terminal_outcomes: One positive owner-ledger adjustment restores the amount owed
One balanced derived adjustment journal exists
Replay does not duplicate the reversal or journal
invariants: Scope is inherited from the original charge
Principal and fee are separately idempotent
happy_path: Validate signed event and terminal provider state
alternate_paths: Won dispute leaves the payment intact
failure_paths: Duplicate reversal re-runs projection without a second adjustment

retention: Original payment and reversal remain separately attributable

async_steps: continuity sweep if projection is interrupted

notifications.failure_handling: Projection failure is visible through WF-PAY-003

tenant_scope: Association, unit, and person are inherited from the original recorded charge
privacy: No new provider payload or private details are copied into alerts

logs: Stripe reversal source and stable reference

compensation: Reversal is an attributable adjustment rather than mutation of the original payment
manual_recovery: Protected canonical repair when persisted journal shape is noncanonical

evidence.claim: Refund and lost-dispute reversals are scoped, state-guarded, idempotent, and re-project accounting
Adjustment journals feed the derived ledger and statement model
